topic: A person's online friend turns out to live in the same city
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up again, a cheerful, brightly colored banner announcing ‘Liam has arrived in the city!’ I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to vibrate the stale air of my apartment. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening. Liam, my online confidant for three years, the one whose profound philosophical musings I cherished, was apparently sharing my zip code. I rubbed my temples, the familiar dull throb behind my eyes intensifying. I picked up my mug, the lukewarm coffee suddenly tasting like ash. "Seriously?" I muttered to the empty room, pacing the length of the rug. The sheer audacity of it—the digital fantasy shattering into mundane reality—made my shoulders slump in a heavy defeat.

[story 2]

I dropped my phone onto the worn armchair as if it had suddenly become radioactive. The screen glowed accusingly, displaying Liam’s new location pin, right here, in my neighborhood. A sharp, involuntary exhale escaped my lips. I stood up, walking over to the window, pulling the blinds shut against the intrusion of the afternoon sun. My hands clenched into fists at my sides, the knuckles turning white. Why? Why did this have to happen now? I let out a low, frustrated groan, running a hand roughly through my hair. It felt like a setup, a cruel cosmic joke designed solely to test my tolerance for the absurd.

[story 3]

"No, no, no," I repeated, the words sounding hollow even to my own ears. I leaned back against the doorframe, letting my gaze drift from the notification back to the device. My breathing became shallow, quick bursts of air catching in my chest. I started tapping a frantic rhythm on the desk, a restless energy bubbling beneath my skin. It was impossible. This wasn't a glitch; this was actual, inconvenient proximity. I pushed my chair back with unnecessary force, the scrape of metal against the floor echoing the sudden tightness in my throat. I just wanted the screen to go dark.
